DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANTS LEONARD ALBERT, LUXOR CABS, INC. (ERRONEOUSLY SUED AND SERVED) AMENDED NOTICE OF MOTION TO COMPEL MENTAL EXAM OF PLAINTIFF SHANA COOPER IS DENIED. DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT DEMONSTRATED THAT PLAINTIFF SEEKS RECOVERY FOR MENTAL AND EMOTIONAL DISTRESS OVER AND ABOVE THAT USUALLY ASSOCIATED WITH THE PHYSICAL INJURIES CLAIMED. FURTHER, THE COURT FINDS THAT PLAINTIFF DOESE NOT SEEK RECOVERY FOR MENTAL AND EMOTIONAL DISTRESS OVER AND ABOVE THAT USUALLY ASSOCIATED WITH THE PHYSICAL INJURIES CLAIMED AND DOES NOT INTEND TO RELY AT TRIAL, IN SUPPORT OF A DAMAGES CLAIM, ON EXPERT TESTIMONY REGARDING THE USUAL MENTAL AND EMOTIONAL DISTRESS ASSOCIATED WITH PLAINTIFF'S PHYSICAL INJURIES. ORDER IS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS).JUDGE PRO TEM: GEOFF HOWARD, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, MELINDA MARTIN, CSR# 9240, (559-636-1306)REPORTED. (302/JPT)

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Mental Examination Of Pltf Michael Sims
Off Calendar Apr-25-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Casey Kaufman, Esq of the Brandi Law Firm (415-989-1800)for Shana Cooper, Plaintiff and opposing party.
Robin Krutzsch, Esq of Wood Smith Henning & Berman LLP (925-356-8200) for Luxor Cab Inc and Leonard Albert, Defendants and moving parties.
Melinda Martin, Court Reporter, CSR# 9240, (559-636-1306).

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTs LEONARD ALBERT, LUXOR CABS, INC.'s (ERRONEOUSLY SUED AND SERVED) Motion To Compel Mental Examination Of Plaintiff Michael Sims is Off calendar. Duplicate entry.Judge Pro Tem: Geoff Howard, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported.=(302/JPT)

Amended Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Mental Examination Of Pltf Michael Sims
Off Calendar Apr-25-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Casey Kaufman, Esq of the Brandi Law Firm (415-989-1800)for Shana Cooper, Plaintiff and opposing party.
Robin Krutzsch, Esq of Wood Smith Henning & Berman LLP (925-356-8200) for Luxor Cab Inc and Leonard Albert, Defendants and moving parties.
Melinda Martin, Court Reporter, CSR# 9240, (559-636-1306).

Argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows with modifications: DEFENDANTs LEONARD ALBERT, LUXOR CABS, INC.'s (ERRONEOUSLY SUED AND SERVED) Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Mental Examination Of Plaintiff Michael Sims is Denied. Defendants have not demonstrated that Plaintiff seeks recovery for mental and emotional distress over and above that usually associated with the physical injuries claimed. Further, the Court finds that Plaintiff does not seek recovery for mental and emotional distress over and above that usually associated with the physical injuries claimed and does not intend to rely at trial, in support of a damages claim, on expert testimony regarding the usual mental and emotional distress associated with Plaintiff's physical injuries. Order is signed in open Court. (See scanned order for details).Judge Pro Tem: Geoff Howard, Clerk: M. Goodman, Melinda Martin, CSR# 9240, (559-636-1306) Reported. (302/JPT)
